key_departures.impact: Bush was the Browns' lone defensive starting-caliber free agent. Posted career highs of 125 tackles, 3 INTs, 2 forced fumbles, 2 sacks, and 2 defensive TDs in 2025. Lost to the Bears on a 3-year, $30M deal. Significant hole at the position, but Browns moved quickly to replace him.

Njoku announced his departure via social media before free agency opened. Posted just 293 receiving yards in 12 games in 2025 in a diminished role behind 2025 draft pick Harold Fannin Jr. His departure creates a TE2/blocking TE need, though Fannin mitigates the loss at TE1.

Teller posted his own farewell before free agency. Was a 3-time Pro Bowler but production declined in 2025 with injuries limiting him. One of four starting OL hitting free agency simultaneously. Browns aggressively replaced interior line depth.

Acquisition of Tytus Howard signals Conklin will not return. Conklin battled significant knee injuries throughout his tenure in Cleveland and was no longer the same player by the end of his time with the team. Right tackle vacancy effectively replaced by Howard trade.

Starting left tackle is now a glaring vacancy with Robinson departing. No replacement has been signed at LT as of March 10, making this the most critical remaining need on the roster. Browns are expected to address LT in the draft.

Pocic suffered a torn Achilles in Week 14 and his contract voided. Coming off major injury at 31, Browns expected to move on. Center remains a need though Elgton Jenkins (C/G) and Luke Wypler provide stopgap options.

7-time Pro Bowler mulling retirement. Contract void date pushed to March 10-11. Browns' signing of Zion Johnson at Bitonio's LG position signals they are prepared for his departure. If he retires, Browns absorb a ~$23.5M dead cap hit. His departure is effectively replaced by the Johnson signing.

Rotational DE signed a 1-year, $3.1M deal with Atlanta. Had 310 defensive snaps with Cleveland in 2025, posting a sack in a memorable Week 12 win. Depth loss but not a starter. Minimal impact on overall need picture.

Missed all of 2025 with a torn Achilles. Browns have Denzel Ward and Tyson Campbell as starting CBs. Emerson played primarily nickel. His departure is not expected to create a significant hole given the existing depth at the position.

signings.analysis: Acquired via trade (5th-round pick to Houston) and immediately signed to a 3-year, $63M extension with $43M guaranteed. Howard has started all 93 games he has played and is highly versatile, having played RT, RG, and LG. Expected to start at right tackle, directly replacing Conklin. The multi-year extension with substantial guarantees signals this is Cleveland's RT for the foreseeable future. Draft compensation: Browns sent a 5th-round pick to Houston. RT need fully addressed; only LT remains on the OT front.
signings.trade_details: Browns sent 5th-round pick to Houston Texans

signings.analysis: 3-year, $49.5M deal with $32.4M guaranteed (including $17.1M signing bonus plus 2026 and 2027 salaries fully guaranteed, per OverTheCap). Chargers' first-round pick in 2022 who started 65 games. He was the top guard on the free agent market and ranked 2nd in run block win rate (79.3%) in 2025. Pass-blocking concerns remain (5th-lowest pass block win rate among guards), but the run-blocking fit for Monken's Ravens-style scheme is excellent. The deal includes 5 void years for cap management, making the real cap hit ~$5M in 2026 and ~$8M in 2027. Fills one of the two guard vacancies (likely LG), but IOL still needs resolution at RG (Teven Jenkins competition) and C (Elgton Jenkins/Wypler). IOL partially addressed, not fully — LG secured, C and RG still unsettled.

2-year, $24M deal with $20M guaranteed (83% of total value guaranteed), per NFL Network's Tom Pelissero. Two-time Pro Bowler (2020, 2022) at left guard who moved to center in 2025 before fracturing his leg and suffering ligament damage in Week 9. Released by Green Bay with a failed-physical designation. Likely slots at center, filling the Pocic vacancy, though he could also play guard. The Browns and Ravens (who lost Linderbaum) were both pursuing Jenkins, likely driving up the guarantee. Coming off a significant injury, the failed physical tag introduces health risk. With Johnson at LG and Jenkins at C, Cleveland now has four of five OL starters filled. Only LT remains as a critical hole. Combined with Johnson and Howard, IOL need is substantially addressed though not fully — health risk and LT/RG competition still linger.

Re-signed to a 1-year deal (terms not fully disclosed; contract void from prior deal was $1.2M dead cap). Appeared in all 17 games in 2025, starting four at RG late in the season when Teller was injured. Had a 94.4% pass block win rate at guard when he played (would have ranked 17th), but run block win rate was among the worst (65.6%). Projects as a RG starter or backup depending on draft adds. One-year deal signals a bridge/competition player rather than the long-term answer. Depth and competition but not a slam-dunk starter.

2-year deal worth up to $17M (NFL.com reported $13.1M base per Judy Battista; Jordan Schultz reported 'up to $17M' — using the upper figure as reported by most outlets). Reunites with new DC Mike Rutenberg, who coached Williams with the Jets LBs from 2021-24. First-team All-Pro in 2023 (139 tackles, 15 TFLs, 2 sacks, 1 INT). In 2025, battled a shoulder injury, missed 4 games, and was briefly demoted under Aaron Glenn. Turned 30 in August 2026. The 2-year deal is a real commitment but at below-LB starter market AAV (market starter tier is $8-16M, so this is low-end starter). He replaces Devin Bush next to reigning DROY Carson Schwesinger. The 2-year structure and age (30 in August) means LB remains a long-term need for the draft, but the immediate starter hole is filled.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: RT fully addressed by Howard trade/extension (3yr/$63M, $43M gtd). However, LEFT TACKLE remains a critical, gaping hole — Cam Robinson departed and no LT has been signed. The right side is solved; the left side is not. OT as a composite position drops from tier 1 only because RT is secured. LT specifically should be treated as a tier 1 need heading into the draft. Browns have picks 6 and 24 and are heavily expected to draft an LT in round 1.

Three significant IOL moves: Howard (versatile G/T), Zion Johnson (3yr/$49.5M, LG starter), and Elgton Jenkins (2yr/$24M, C starter). Teven Jenkins re-signed as depth/RG competition. The interior is substantially rebuilt. However, health risk on Elgton Jenkins (failed physical designation coming off fractured leg + ligament damage) and uncertainty at RG (Teven Jenkins vs. draft pick) keep this from being fully resolved. Bitonio's expected departure further muddles the picture. Grade down from tier 1 to tier 2 — Browns should still target OL depth in the mid-rounds.

Devin Bush (3yr/$30M to Bears) departure escalated the need, but Browns answered quickly with Quincy Williams (2yr/$17M). Williams is an established starter with DC Rutenberg familiarity and a first-team All-Pro pedigree. The 2-year deal at age 30 means the long-term need still exists, but the immediate starter vacancy is filled. Schwesinger + Williams is a legitimate starting pair. LB drops from tier 2 to tier 3 — developmental depth in mid-to-late rounds would be appropriate, but no longer a premium need.

David Njoku's departure creates a TE2/blocking TE void. Harold Fannin Jr. is the clear TE1, but Cleveland needs a complementary tight end. Todd Monken's offense at Baltimore was heavily two-TE, and no replacement for Njoku has been signed. The Browns have been linked to Charlie Kolar (Ravens) given the Monken connection. TE escalates to tier 2 — a day 2-3 draft pick or late free agent signing is needed to complete the room.

No WR additions made through March 10. The Browns' WR room produced only 1,467 receiving yards in 2025 — the least productive in the NFL. Top target Alec Pierce signed with the Colts (4yr/$116M). Cleveland was reportedly 'aggressively in the mix' for Alijah Vera-Tucker (who went to New England). WR remains a critical tier 1 need. Expected to be addressed heavily in the draft (picks 6, 24, 39 available) and potentially via trade.

No QB additions. Malik Willis signed with the Dolphins ($67.5M), Tua Tagovailoa went to Atlanta. Shedeur Sanders (rookie) and Deshaun Watson (returning from Achilles, $80.7M restructured cap hit) are the options. Kyler Murray and Geno Smith are being released and could be targets. QB remains a critical tier 1 need — the starter competition between Sanders and Watson is unresolved, and a veteran bridge QB may still be added.

No EDGE signings or departures of starters. Cameron Thomas (rotational) left for Atlanta on a 1yr/$3.1M deal — depth loss only. Myles Garrett remains under contract and is the franchise cornerstone. Need unchanged.

Denzel Ward and Tyson Campbell (acquired via trade from Jacksonville in 2025) are under contract as the starting corners. Martin Emerson Jr. (missed all of 2025 with Achilles) is the only CB departure and was not a starter. CB need unchanged — depth/nickel work may be addressed in later draft rounds but no priority concern.

key_additions.headline: Versatile veteran RT acquired via trade anchors massive OL overhaul; starts all 93 career games
Top free-agent guard slots in at LG, ranked 2nd in run block win rate in 2025 for Monken's run-heavy scheme
Two-time Pro Bowl OL (C/G) fills Pocic's center vacancy; health risk after fractured leg/ligament damage in 2025
Former All-Pro LB reunites with DC Rutenberg to replace Devin Bush alongside DROY Carson Schwesinger
Re-signed OG/OT provides depth and RG competition in Cleveland's revamped interior line room

key_losses.headline: Starter-caliber LB (125 tackles, 3 INTs in 2025) departs; quickly replaced by Quincy Williams
Nine-year Brown and former Pro Bowler departs after farewell post; Harold Fannin Jr. takes TE1 role
7-time Pro Bowl LG likely retiring; replaced by Zion Johnson signing in a planned succession
3-time Pro Bowl RG posts farewell; part of unprecedented all-5-starter OL turnover in one offseason
Starting LT departs with no replacement yet signed; left tackle remains the Browns' most critical remaining need
Starting C departs after torn Achilles in Week 14; Elgton Jenkins acquired to fill the vacancy
Injury-plagued RT departs; directly replaced by Tytus Howard trade from Houston

additional_context.ol_overhaul_note: No team since 1970 has lost all five starting OL in the same offseason (per CBS Sports Research). Cleveland replaced RT (Howard), LG (Zion Johnson), C (Elgton Jenkins), and RG competition (Teven Jenkins), but LEFT TACKLE remains completely unaddressed as of March 10. This is the most glaring remaining hole on the roster and will almost certainly be addressed in the top half of the 2026 NFL Draft (Browns hold picks 6 and 24).
additional_context.watson_cap_note: Deshaun Watson's contract was restructured to lower his $80.7M cap hit. He missed all of 2025 recovering from a torn Achilles (2024) and is expected to compete for the starting job in 2026 alongside Shedeur Sanders.
additional_context.draft_capital: Browns hold picks: Round 1 (#6, #24), Round 2 (#39), Round 3 (#70), Round 4 (#107), Round 5 (#145, #148), Round 6 (#205), Round 7 (#248). Lost one 5th-round pick in the Tytus Howard trade.
